examples: The president insisted that he was acting out of compassion, not political opportunism.
The senator responded by saying that the opposition's concern for the environment was political opportunism.
Our ancestors adapted to the universal but irregular global warming since the end of the last great Ice Age, around 10,000 years ago, with dazzling opportunism.
Riots continue in London, fueled by a combination of poverty, police resentment and criminal opportunism.
Wednesday's deal also represents a certain degree of opportunism on the part of Spain.
